label: Wide-shell 2BR / 1 bath — L-SHAPE GREAT ROOM with mid-band HALL. Master at front-left has a direct door east into the great room (no hall buffer for master). Standard at rear-left accesses the great room via the hall, which sits in the middle band between standard and the common T&B. The great room is generously sized — it spans the full right column from front to where the kitchen takes over at the rear, plus extends west into the middle band south of the kitchen, taking the area between hall and kitchen.

adjacencies.note: master (front) and standard (rear) stack vertically in the left column — shared horizontal wall only, no door between them.

master's north wall has a door opening north into the hall — master's primary public access. Master enters via the hall (not directly into the great room) so the hall becomes the shared bedroom-wing circulation node. The horizontal wall between master (south) and hall (north) sits at master.y_end = standard.y_start = hall.y_start; the door is on the eastern portion of master's north wall where master overhangs east of standard.

master's east wall = great room's west wall — geometry-only constraint with NO door. This pins great's west boundary to master's east boundary (master.x_end = great.x_start) so the building outline stays a clean rectangle at the front band, no gap between master and great. Master accesses public space via the hall (north door); this wall is purely for layout alignment. 2.0 m min keeps master's east face full-height against great.
standard's east wall has a door opening east into the hall — standard's only public access. Standard reaches the great room via the hall.

standard's east wall touches common's west wall at the rear band — shared wall only, no door. Standard's door (on standard's east wall) opens at the south end of this wall into the hall portion; the wall above the door is shared with the common bath.
common bath's south wall opens south into the hall — the single bath's only door. Both bedrooms reach the bath via the hall. DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' door-host group.

common bath shares its east wall with kitchen's west wall — wet stacks consolidate in one rear bay. ALTERNATE host of the 'common_access' group: door placed here only when shared wall >= 1.5 m; falls back to hall on compact lots.

hall's east wall is an open mouth into the great room (no door — codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open-plan). 0.9 m mouth — PH minimum for single-person flow; the compact 14x10 envelope can't accommodate a wider mouth without breaking the y-budget.
great_room + kitchen form one open LDK (gr style). Great spans the front + middle of the right side; kitchen sits at the rear with a wide open boundary to great.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_wd_l_wrap_bath_hall_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: On compact wide lots (<75 sqm buildable, e.g. 14x10 m = 60 sqm), kitchen depth floors at 1.8 m for galley work-triangle clearance. Hall depth at 1.0 m hits PH minimum corridor, capped at 3 sqm so it doesn't absorb area from the great room. Common bath at preferred range 3.5-4.5 sqm.

notes: Wide-shell 2BR / 1 bath topology with master-direct-to-great and standard-via-hall access patterns. Designed against 14x10 m (60 sqm) as the canonical compact case. Produces a much more generous great_room than 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr (which routes BOTH bedrooms through the hall and therefore loses area to the hall).
Front band:   [MASTER BR      |       GREAT ROOM (front + middle) ]
- LEFT  (~3-3.5 m): [master front, standard rear] — bedroom column
- MID   (~2 m):    [hall front-of-rear-band, common T&B at rear]
- RIGHT (~5 m):    [great_room front + middle, kitchen rear] — LDK column
Master access: direct door east into great room (master.east_wall = great.west_wall in the front band). No buffer; master is one wall away from the public LDK.
Standard access: door east into hall (standard.east_wall = hall.west_wall in the middle band). Hall opens east into great room (open mouth, no door).
Common bath access: south door into hall. Both bedrooms reach the bath via the hall.
Wet stack: common bath's east wall = kitchen's west wall (wet_core). All wet utilities (kitchen sink, bath, dirty kitchen) consolidate in one rear-east bay.
- Living + Dining combined into one open great_room
- Kitchen stacked behind great_room at the rear-right (right column)
- Great_room can extend west into the middle band area south of kitchen — the open plan boundary great↔kitchen lets the solver/snap_gaps grow great_room into this strip naturally

Carport: not declared in the base topology — NCP form. To add a side carport, add a building_void at front_right. For front-parallel carport, brief.carport_side='front' bumps the front setback to 3 m. When brief.carport_side='left', the runner mirrors the topology (LDK on left, bedrooms on right).
Fallback: 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr — the version where BOTH bedrooms route through the hall (smaller great_room but works on tighter shells).
